item.title: Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment for AI video
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Is this just normal corporate strategy, or are we about to see a broader pullback on AI-generated video?

item.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

item.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

item.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

item.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

item.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

item.title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]

item.title: OpenAI shuts down Sora while Meta gets shut out in court
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]

item.title: David Sacks is done as AI czar — here’s what he’s doing instead
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Sacks will be much further from the power center in Washington than since the outset of this second Trump administration.

item.title: Anthropic wins injunction against Trump administration over Defense Department saga
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: A federal judge has ordered that the Trump administration rescind recent restrictions it placed on the AI company.

item.title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

item.title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

item.title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

item.title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

item.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

item.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

item.title: Mistral releases a new open source model for speech generation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The model, which lets enterprises build voice agents for sales and customer engagement, puts Mistral in direct competition with the likes of ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI.
